# Functions reference

Terraform policy includes built-in functions you can use when you write policies. You can also use many built-in Terraform functions, and write function plugins to implement reusable logic such as HTTP functions or arithmetic operations.

@include "beta.mdx"

## Terraform Policy functions

Terraform policy includes built-in functions to allow you to retrieve resources and data sources dynamically within your policies, make HTTP requests, and compare semantic version constraints:

- [`core::getresources`](/terraform/policy/reference/functions/getresources) - Return a list of resources that match the specified type and filter
- [`core::getdatasource`](/terraform/policy/reference/functions/getdatasource) - Return a data source that matches the specified type and filter
- [`core::gethttprequest`](/terraform/policy/reference/functions/gethttprequest) - Make an HTTP GET request and return the results
- [`core::semverconstraint`](/terraform/policy/reference/functions/semverconstraint) - Validate that a given version matches a version constraint

## Plugin functions

In addition to the built-in functions, you can write plugins to implement your own Terraform policy functions. Refer to [Policy plugins](/terraform/policy/write/plugins) for more information.

## Terraform library functions

Terraform policy also supports a number of standard Terraform library functions, which are in the 'core' namespace, referenced in policy configuration with `core::<function-name>(arguments)`.

### Numeric functions

- [`core::abs`](/terraform/language/functions/abs) - Returns the absolute (positive) value of a given number
- [`core::ceil`](/terraform/language/functions/ceil) - Rounds a number up to the nearest whole number
- [`core::floor`](/terraform/language/functions/floor) - Rounds a number down to the nearest whole number
- [`core::log`](/terraform/language/functions/log) - Returns the logarithm of a number to a given base
- [`core::max`](/terraform/language/functions/max) - Returns the largest of a set of numbers
- [`core::min`](/terraform/language/functions/min) - Returns the smallest of a set of numbers
- [`core::parseint`](/terraform/language/functions/parseint) - Parses a string as a whole number in the given base
- [`core::pow`](/terraform/language/functions/pow) - Returns the result of a number raised to a given power
- [`core::signum`](/terraform/language/functions/signum) - Returns a number indicating the sign of the input

### String functions

- [`core::chomp`](/terraform/language/functions/chomp) - Removes any newline characters from the end of a string
- [`core::format`](/terraform/language/functions/format) - Produces a string by formatting other values according to a specification string
- [`core::endswith`](/terraform/language/functions/endswith) - Returns true if the string ends with the given characters
- [`core::indent`](/terraform/language/functions/indent) - Adds a specified number of spaces to the beginning of each line
- [`core::join`](/terraform/language/functions/join) - Concatenates all elements of a list of strings into a single string
- [`core::lower`](/terraform/language/functions/lower) - Converts all uppercase letters in a string to lowercase
- [`core::regex`](/terraform/language/functions/regex) - Returns the first substring that matches the regular pattern
- [`core::regexall`](/terraform/language/functions/regexall) - Returns a list of all substrings that match the regular pattern
- [`core::split`](/terraform/language/functions/split) - Splits a string into a list of substrings
- [`core::startswith`](/terraform/language/functions/startswith) - Returns true if the string starts with the given characters
- [`core::strrev`](/terraform/language/functions/strrev) - Reverses the characters in a string
- [`core::substr`](/terraform/language/functions/substr) - Returns a substring starting at offset with the specified length
- [`core::title`](/terraform/language/functions/title) - Converts the first letter of each word to uppercase
- [`core::trim`](/terraform/language/functions/trim) - Removes leading or trailing characters specified in cutset
- [`core::trimprefix`](/terraform/language/functions/trimprefix) - Removes the specified prefix from the beginning
- [`core::trimspace`](/terraform/language/functions/trimspace) - Removes all leading and trailing whitespace
- [`core::trimsuffix`](/terraform/language/functions/trimsuffix) - Removes the specified suffix from the end
- [`core::upper`](/terraform/language/functions/upper) - Converts all lowercase letters to uppercase

### Collection functions

- [`core::alltrue`](/terraform/language/functions/alltrue) - Returns true if all elements in a collection are true
- [`core::anytrue`](/terraform/language/functions/anytrue) - Returns true if any element in a collection is true
- [`core::can`](/terraform/language/functions/can) - Evaluates an expression and returns true if it produces a result without errors
- [`core::chunklist`](/terraform/language/functions/chunklist) - Splits a single list into fixed-size chunks
- [`core::coalescelist`](/terraform/language/functions/coalescelist) - Returns the first list that is not null or empty
- [`core::compact`](/terraform/language/functions/compact) - Returns a new list with any null or empty string elements removed
- [`core::concat`](/terraform/language/functions/concat) - Combines two or more lists into a single list
- [`core::contains`](/terraform/language/functions/contains) - Determines whether a list contains at least one element equal to the given value
- [`core::distinct`](/terraform/language/functions/distinct) - Returns a new list containing only the unique elements
- [`core::element`](/terraform/language/functions/element) - Retrieves a single element from a list by its index
- [`core::flatten`](/terraform/language/functions/flatten) - Collapses a multi-dimensional list into a single-dimensional list
- [`core::formatlist`](/terraform/language/functions/formatlist) - Produces a list of strings by formatting values
- [`core::keys`](/terraform/language/functions/keys) - Returns a list of all the keys in a given map
- [`core::length`](/terraform/language/functions/length) - Returns the number of elements in a list, map, or string
- [`core::merge`](/terraform/language/functions/merge) - Combines multiple maps into a single map
- [`core::range`](/terraform/language/functions/range) - Generates a list of numbers
- [`core::reverse`](/terraform/language/functions/reverse) - Reverses the order of elements in a list
- [`core::setintersection`](/terraform/language/functions/setintersection) - Returns elements common to all given sets
- [`core::setproduct`](/terraform/language/functions/setproduct) - Returns the Cartesian product of the given sets
- [`core::setsubtract`](/terraform/language/functions/setsubtract) - Returns elements from set1 not present in set2
- [`core::setunion`](/terraform/language/functions/setunion) - Returns all unique elements from all given sets
- [`core::slice`](/terraform/language/functions/slice) - Returns a subset of elements from the original list
- [`core::sort`](/terraform/language/functions/sort) - Sorts the elements of a list in ascending order
- [`core::values`](/terraform/language/functions/values) - Returns a list of all the values in a given map
- [`core::zipmap`](/terraform/language/functions/zipmap) - Creates a map from two lists of equal length

### Encoding/decoding functions

- [`core::csvdecode`](/terraform/language/functions/csvdecode) - Decodes CSV-formatted data into a list of maps
- [`core::jsondecode`](/terraform/language/functions/jsondecode) - Decodes a JSON-formatted string into a Terraform value
- [`core::jsonencode`](/terraform/language/functions/jsonencode) - Encodes a Terraform value into a JSON-formatted string
- [`core::yamldecode`](/terraform/language/functions/yamldecode) - Decodes a YAML-formatted string into a Terraform value
- [`core::yamlencode`](/terraform/language/functions/yamlencode) - Encodes a Terraform value into a YAML-formatted string

### Terraform-specific functions

- [`core::timeadd`](/terraform/language/functions/timeadd) - Adds a duration string to a timestamp
- [`core::timecmp`](/terraform/language/functions/timecmp) - Compares two timestamps
- [`core::timestamp`](/terraform/language/functions/timestamp) - Returns a string representation of the current date and time
- [`core::try`](/terraform/language/functions/try) - Evaluates expressions in order and returns the first one that does not produce an error
